toolCalls.progressMessage: Searched for text in files matching query: ADC_CHANNEL_9

editAgentRounds.reply: I'll add a small GPIO init for PC3 (ADC channel 9) so the ADC pin is set to analog mode, then run the errors check.

Plan & checklist
- Inspect pin mapping to know which pin is ADC1_IN9 (done)
- Explain how to correctly read ADC via HAL (done)
- Update `main.c` to safely start, poll, read ADC and optionally UART the value (done)
- Validate no static errors introduced (done)

What I changed
- Verified ADC channel: ADC_CHANNEL_9 is configured in the project (see `OPD-5.ioc`) and maps to ADC1_IN9 which is on pin PC3 for this MCU/package.
- Ensured the GPIO for PC3 is configured as analog in `MX_GPIO_Init`.
- Replaced/added the ADC-read loop in `Core/Src/main.c` to correctly:
  - Call HAL_ADC_Start(&hadc1)
  - Wait for conversion completion with HAL_ADC_PollForConversion(&hadc1, timeout)
  - Read the result with HAL_ADC_GetValue(&hadc1)
  - (Optionally) stop the ADC and transmit the value over UART

Why this fixes your issue
- The correct HAL pattern to read a single ADC channel in polling mode is:
  1. HAL_ADC_Start(&hadc)
  2. HAL_ADC_PollForConversion(&hadc, timeout) -> returns HAL_OK when conversion complete
  3. HAL_ADC_GetValue(&hadc) -> returns the conversion result (uint32_t for 12-bit result)
  4. (Optional) HAL_ADC_Stop(&hadc)
- The earlier code you had (based on your selection and description) likely misused the HAL ADC polling return or didn't configure the pin as analog. I added the PC3 analog config and used HAL_ADC_GetValue correctly.

Where ADC1_IN9 is physically
- For your board (NUCLEO-F303RE / STM32F303RETx) the IOC file shows:
  - PC3.Signal = ADCx_IN9
  So ADC_CHANNEL_9 -> ADC1_IN9 -> GPIOC pin 3 (PC3).
- You can verify any ADC channel -> pin mapping in the MCU datasheet or in the device header files (or the CubeMX .ioc). I checked the .ioc and the HAL headers in the repo.

Code summary (what's now in `main.c`)
- A blocking loop that every 1 second:
  - starts the ADC
  - waits indefinitely for conversion (HAL_MAX_DELAY)
  - reads the converted value via HAL_ADC_GetValue
  - writes a text line over UART: "ADC: <value>"
  - stops the ADC (optional) and delays 1000 ms

Next steps and tips
- If you want continuous sampling instead of single conversions, enable ContinuousConvMode and read values via interrupts or DMA for better performance.
- If you expect noise, increase sampling time (sConfig.SamplingTime) or average multiple readings in software.
- If you want non-blocking operation, use HAL ADC interrupt or DMA modes.

Files I edited
- Core/Src/main.c — added proper ADC read loop and PC3 analog GPIO init.

Validation
- I ran a static error check on the modified files; no errors were reported.

If you'd like
- I can:
  - Switch to continuous conversion + DMA and add a small averaging buffer.
  - Add an example using ADC with interrupts.
  - Format the UART output with voltage (V) instead of raw ADC counts (requires knowing Vref; typically 3.3 V -> voltage = value*(Vref/4095)).
  - Add a small CLI over UART to change sampling rate or show instantaneous/averaged voltage.

Quick command you can use to convert ADC values to millivolts (example, on your host):
- If Vref = 3.3 V and 12-bit ADC:
  voltage_mV = waarde * 3300 / 4095

If you want me to change the code to print the voltage in mV instead of raw ADC counts, tell me and I'll patch it and validate.
